commentary: Catered my first 200-cover wedding this weekend with Cuisine du Nord. Tourtiere, sugar pie, the whole spread. Twelve hours on my feet and I would do it again tomorrow. Merci to the team.
A hospitality lesson I keep relearning: people don't remember the room, they remember how you made them feel when something went wrong. Concierge desk or catering kitchen, same rule.
Sourcing maple and specialty goods straight from Quebec suppliers for the catering business. If you're in PDX and want real tourtiere for your next event, let's talk.
Moved from Boston to Portland a few years back not knowing a soul. This week a guest at The Hawthorne Grand told me I'm the reason they keep coming back. That's the whole job.
Running a catering company alongside a full-time concierge role is a masterclass in time management. Prep lists save lives.
Grateful for the Portland food community. Saturday Market vendors, fellow caterers, the whole scene. You lift each other up here.
Booked out the holiday season for Cuisine du Nord. Reveillon menus, corporate parties, family gatherings. Quebecois comfort food travels well.
To everyone in hospitality working the long weekend so others can rest: I see you. The work matters.
Two hundred covers plated at the Whitfield wedding this weekend. Kimchi tourtière + sugar pie + maple crème brûlée. Twelve hours on my feet. Would do it again tomorrow.
Cuisine du Nord Instagram hit 4,300 followers. Grateful to the Portland food community for the referrals and the word-of-mouth.
Réveillon menu drop sold out in five hours. Thirty-two seats for a chef's tasting Dec 20. Never happened before at that speed.
Culinary Crossroads shared kitchen tour tomorrow. Big decision. Twenty hours a week at $800/month. Would love to hear from anyone using shared commissary space in Portland.
Ambervale Properties corporate lunch Nov 13 booked. 40 pax, kimchi tourtière + poutine bar + tarte au sucre miniatures. First recurring corporate contract of the year.
Grand-père Henri's 80th birthday in Montréal August 2027. Flights booked PDX-YUL. Family + parish + Chez Léveillé. All three generations of Quinn men in the same room.
Melissa Ostrander CPA is starting on Cuisine du Nord's books in January. Ten years of Patrick Doyle telling me to hire a real accountant. He was right.
Silverpeak Credit Union pre-approved my $52,000 food truck loan at 7.9% APR. SBA microloan still processing. Momentum matters.
Corporate holiday party Dec 12 confirmed — 120 covers, smoked duck tourtière signature, cretons canapés, maple crème brûlée. Biggest ticket in Cuisine du Nord history.
Aspirant Les Clefs d'Or member update — three-month mentor reflection with Rita Vlahos at The Nines was one of the most useful hours of the year.
Sold out the November Sunday supper pop-up — chef's tasting for 12, all locals via Eventbrite. The Sunday supper series is here to stay.
Ambervale corporate lunch Nov 13 debrief — 40 covers, four-course, kimchi tourtière went to the top of the request list. Locking a recurring quarterly cadence.
Whitfield wedding Chehalem Ridge tasting confirmed. Cretons canapés + kimchi tourtière + maple crème brûlée. Signed contract in DocuSign. Deposit received.
Portland Saturday Market booth F-14 is my most consistent revenue channel. Thankful for the winter market community showing up rain or shine.
Papa Luc rolled 60 pâte brisée disks by hand this Saturday. Watching a 62-year-old carpenter turn into a pastry chef in real time is the whole reason for this business.
Émile Quinn — my brother — has served at every catering event since Cuisine du Nord started. $100 an event and he's still the most reliable staff I've ever had.
Sienna Park at Copperstone Grill is the sous chef half of this operation's brain. The kimchi tourtière is her fingerprint. All the fusion ideas start with her.
Reddit r/foodtruck thread this month — 'Silverpeak vs SBA microloan' had 34 replies. The Portland food entrepreneur community online is generous.
Culinary Crossroads shared commercial kitchen — 20 hours a week at $800/month. Signed the license agreement. Making the jump from cottage food to commissary next quarter.
Melissa Ostrander CPA — first-year engagement letter signed. QuickBooks migration in January. Ten years of Patrick Doyle pushing this call finally paid off.
Cuisine du Nord LLC formation in progress. General liability, commercial auto, workers' comp for Émile and Sienna — real business infrastructure taking shape.
Sunday supper pop-up series expanded from 12 seats to 24 for December. All tickets via Eventbrite, all sold before the newsletter went out.
Cuisine du Nord Google Analytics — Webflow site traffic doubled month-over-month after the November Instagram content plan launch. Content works.
Instagram-to-booking conversion rate on the Ambervale corporate lunch post is 12%. The 4,300-follower audience is small but the intent-to-book is real.
The Hawthorne Grand concierge team — Marc, Priya, Jess — the four of us have booked $180k in guest reservations combined this quarter. Portland hospitality works.
